summary: Created official Codex cron automations for codex-supervisor queue reconciliation and project health reporting through the Codex automation tool, verified the local automation records, and recorded planning/handoff evidence without writing Codex internal SQLite databases.

tests_run.summary: Story Loop status returned queue_state=completed after Stage 10F completion.
374 passed plus Ruff, format, mypy, CLI smoke checks, file justification, public hygiene, planning integrity, skill inventory, source inventory, protected locks, and uv lock --check.

Existing Codex automations are inspected.evidence: Inspected CODEX_HOME automations before creation; the local automations directory was missing before the official automation tool calls, so no existing local automation TOMLs matched these names.

A recurring queue reconciliation automat.evidence: codex_app.automation_update created automation id codex-supervisor-queue-reconciliation with ACTIVE cron configuration for the configured codex-supervisor workspace and a read-only queue reporting prompt.

A recurring project health check automat.evidence: codex_app.automation_update created automation id codex-supervisor-project-health-check with ACTIVE cron configuration for the configured codex-supervisor workspace and a read-only project health prompt.

Planning SQLite and HANDOFF.md record th.evidence: plans/planning.sqlite3 records the Stage 10F task, worker run, completion progress, milestone, criterion, and worker-result link; HANDOFF.md records automation ids and residual risks; scripts/check_file_justification.py records the worker-result artifact purpose.

No direct writes are made to Codex inter.evidence: Automation creation used the official codex_app.automation_update tool, not raw SQLite writes. Verification includes planning integrity and story-loop-status evidence.

review.summary: Fresh-thread-style local review found no actionable findings. The automations are read-only by prompt, scoped to the codex-supervisor workspace, and avoid direct Codex SQLite writes.

risks: The recurring automations are active external Codex app state and should be updated through official automation tooling, not by editing local TOML files directly.
The automations are intentionally report-only; future slices may add monitored repair task creation only with a separate task contract.
Live Codex Exec launch remains unavailable in this Windows shell because the resolved WindowsApps codex.exe path returns Access is denied.
follow_up_tasks: Shape Stage 11 MCP server work now that Stage 10 local-state and automation bridge slices are complete.
Consider a later automation triage slice that turns repeated automation findings into planning tasks without direct SQLite writes.
Push the local commit stack only after explicit user approval.
handoff_notes: Stage 10F created two ACTIVE official Codex cron automations for codex-supervisor: codex-supervisor-queue-reconciliation and codex-supervisor-project-health-check. Both are local-workspace, read-only reporting automations created through codex_app.automation_update.
